slice.description: Confirm the add(a, b) contract and test framework
acceptance: Verify add(a, b) accepts two numeric arguments and returns a + b.
Select pytest-style one-line `def test_...(): assert ...` form for conciseness.
Decide on the test file location (e.g. tests/test_add.py) mirroring the package path.

expected_output:
{
  "slice_id": "1",
  "diff": "--- a/math_operations/add.py
+++ b/math_operations/add.py
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+def add(a, b):
+    return a + b
+
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/test_add.py
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+import pytest
+from math_operations.add import add
+
def test_add_integers():
+    assert add(2, 3) == 5
+
def test_add_floats():
+    assert abs(add(2.1, 3.2) - 5.3) < 0.0001",
  "notes": "Added the `add` function to math_operations/add.py and created tests/test_add.py with pytest-style one-line assertions for testing integer and float addition."
}
